rationale: The citing paper uses Khan and Dhar (2006) in its conceptual core, not just in a background list. In the behavioral framework, one of the five explicit mechanisms is "licensing/balancing," and the citation is used to justify the prediction that self-control in one part of shopping can lead to later indulgence. That mechanism directly feeds the paper's hypotheses about higher servings and calories from club-store shopping. Even though the mechanism is not separately identified empirically, the citation materially informs theory framing and expected effect direction.
relation_description: The citing paper builds its licensing/balancing mechanism on Khan and Dhar (2006), using it to argue that virtuous or controlled shopping behavior can license indulgent food choices within or after shopping episodes. This conceptual borrowing supports the paper's predicted positive club-store effect on purchased calories and related outcomes.
